symptom_report: Conveyor motor keeps tripping on overload after running for about 20 minutes.
expected_fix_summary: Clear the fan shroud and cooling fins

symptom_report: Line 7 drive motor shuts down on a thermal fault mid-shift, restarts fine once it cools.
expected_fix_summary: Clear the fan shroud and cooling fins

symptom_report: Getting repeated OL trips on the conveyor motor once the line has been running a while.
expected_fix_summary: Clear the fan shroud and cooling fins

symptom_report: VFD is throwing E003 and won't let the line restart.
expected_fix_summary: Clear any mechanical jam on the conveyor bed before re-attempting start

symptom_report: Drive faults out with an overcurrent code right at line start-up, every time.
expected_fix_summary: Clear any mechanical jam on the conveyor bed before re-attempting start

symptom_report: E003 on the VFD display, conveyor won't move at all this morning.
expected_fix_summary: Clear any mechanical jam on the conveyor bed before re-attempting start

symptom_report: Belt seems to be slipping on the drive pulley, panels are arriving late to station 3.
expected_fix_summary: Clean the pulley surface with the approved degreaser if contamination is found

symptom_report: Conveyor speed looks inconsistent even though the VFD readout is steady.
expected_fix_summary: Clean the pulley surface with the approved degreaser if contamination is found

symptom_report: Hearing a rhythmic squeal from the drive end, and the line is running slower than takt.
expected_fix_summary: Clean the pulley surface with the approved degreaser if contamination is found

symptom_report: Line keeps false-stopping like it thinks a panel is there when it isn't.
expected_fix_summary: Realign the sensor bracket using the laser alignment jig and re-torque the mounting screw

symptom_report: Part-present sensor at station 2 is flaky, sometimes it just doesn't see the panel at all.
expected_fix_summary: Realign the sensor bracket using the laser alignment jig and re-torque the mounting screw

symptom_report: Getting random stops with no part jam, sensor light looks like it's flickering.
expected_fix_summary: Realign the sensor bracket using the laser alignment jig and re-torque the mounting screw

symptom_report: Panel clamp isn't holding tight, panels shift slightly during the press cycle.
expected_fix_summary: Replace any cracked line or reseat/replace a loose fitting

symptom_report: Pressure gauge on the clamp unit reads low compared to the shift start checklist value.
expected_fix_summary: Replace any cracked line or reseat/replace a loose fitting

symptom_report: Hearing a faint hiss near the clamp cylinder, and clamp force seems weak.
expected_fix_summary: Replace any cracked line or reseat/replace a loose fitting

symptom_report: HMI keeps showing a comms fault for a second or two, then it clears itself.
expected_fix_summary: Reseat or replace the connector if pins show wear or corrosion

symptom_report: Line hiccups randomly with a PLC timeout error, no pattern to when it happens.
expected_fix_summary: Reseat or replace the connector if pins show wear or corrosion

symptom_report: Getting sporadic communication faults on the control panel, maybe a few times a shift.
expected_fix_summary: Reseat or replace the connector if pins show wear or corrosion

symptom_report: Line trips out right around shift-change every time, like clockwork.
expected_fix_summary: If sag is confirmed and within utility tolerance, stagger Line 7's start-up by 2-3 minutes relative to other lines

symptom_report: We get a nuisance fault every day around the same time in the morning.
expected_fix_summary: If sag is confirmed and within utility tolerance, stagger Line 7's start-up by 2-3 minutes relative to other lines

symptom_report: Drive faults right when the other lines are all starting up together.
expected_fix_summary: If sag is confirmed and within utility tolerance, stagger Line 7's start-up by 2-3 minutes relative to other lines

symptom_report: Not really a fault code, but the drive end sounds different lately, kind of a high whine.
expected_fix_summary: That high, steady whine — not a rattle, not a knock, a whine — is the tell for a bearing starting to go dry. It won't throw any code because load and current bo...
expert_resolution_text: That high, steady whine — not a rattle, not a knock, a whine — is the tell for a bearing starting to go dry. It won't throw any code because load and current both look normal right up until it seizes, sometimes with only a day or two of warning. I put two fingers on the bearing housing (power still on, hand flat, never wrap fingers around anything moving) and if it's noticeably warmer than the opposite side bearing, that confirms it. Swap it on the next planned stop, don't wait for a code, because by the time it faults you're looking at an unplanned line-down and possible shaft damage instead of a 40-minute bearing swap.

symptom_report: Panels have been landing a couple millimeters off at station 4, but it's been gradual, not sudden.
expected_fix_summary: This one's sneaky because it never trips anything — the encoder count itself is fine, it's the coupling between the encoder shaft and the motor shaft that's slo...
expert_resolution_text: This one's sneaky because it never trips anything — the encoder count itself is fine, it's the coupling between the encoder shaft and the motor shaft that's slowly slipping a fraction of a degree at a time from thermal cycling. You won't catch it by looking at the encoder counts in isolation. What I do is compare today's home-position offset against the value recorded in the commissioning sheet — if it's drifted more than about 0.5 degrees, the coupling needs to be re-clamped and the axis re-homed. Left alone it keeps creeping and eventually you get intermittent part rejects that look like a totally unrelated tooling problem.

Left alone it keeps creeping and eventually you get intermittent part rejects that look like a totally unrelated tooling problem.

Left alone it keeps creeping and eventually you get intermittent part rejects that look like a totally unrelated tooling problem.

symptom_report: VFD throws a random fault only when the welding cell next door is running its heavy cycle.
expected_fix_summary: Nobody believes this the first time, but the welding robot cell next door puts harmonic noise back onto the shared supply feed during its high-current weld puls...
expert_resolution_text: Nobody believes this the first time, but the welding robot cell next door puts harmonic noise back onto the shared supply feed during its high-current weld pulses, and our VFD's input filter was never sized for that. It only shows up when both lines are running heavy at the same time, which is why it looks random — it's not random, it's correlated with their cycle, just on a different line so nobody thinks to check it. Fastest confirmation: ask their operator to run one isolated weld cycle while you watch our VFD's DC bus ripple reading. If it spikes in sync, that's your answer. Real fix is an input line reactor on our VFD, but until that's installed, avoid scheduling our high-load changeovers during their heavy weld blocks.

expert_resolution_text: Nobody believes this the first time, but the welding robot cell next door puts harmonic noise back onto the shared supply feed during its high-current weld pulses, and our VFD's input filter was never sized for that. Real fix is an input line reactor on our VFD, but until that's installed, avoid scheduling our high-load changeovers during their heavy weld blocks.

Fastest confirmation: ask their operator to run one isolated weld cycle while you watch our VFD's DC bus ripple reading. If it spikes in sync, that's your answer. Real fix is an input line reactor on our VFD, but until that's installed, avoid scheduling our high-load changeovers during their heavy weld blocks.

symptom_report: Gearbox is running hotter than usual and there's a slightly odd smell near the drive end.
expected_fix_summary: Milky or foamy grease at the breather almost always means someone topped up with the wrong lubricant grade during a changeover — there are two gearboxes on this...
expert_resolution_text: Milky or foamy grease at the breather almost always means someone topped up with the wrong lubricant grade during a changeover — there are two gearboxes on this line that look identical but spec different synthetic greases, and they don't mix well; the wrong one breaks down and foams under heat instead of lubricating properly. If you see that residue, don't just top it up again, that makes it worse. Full drain, flush with the correct grade, and refill to the sight glass line. I'd also tag the grease gun used for that changeover so whoever did it doesn't repeat it on the other line.

I'd also tag the grease gun used for that changeover so whoever did it doesn't repeat it on the other line.

expert_resolution_text: Milky or foamy grease at the breather almost always means someone topped up with the wrong lubricant grade during a changeover — there are two gearboxes on this line that look identical but spec different synthetic greases, and they don't mix well; the wrong one breaks down and foams under heat instead of lubricating properly. I'd also tag the grease gun used for that changeover so whoever did it doesn't repeat it on the other line.

symptom_report: Sensors glitch out only on dry days, seems worse in winter for some reason.
expected_fix_summary: The humidity correlation is the giveaway — that's static, not a sensor defect. The door-panel liner material builds a static charge as it slides across the conv...
expert_resolution_text: The humidity correlation is the giveaway — that's static, not a sensor defect. The door-panel liner material builds a static charge as it slides across the conveyor bed, and it should discharge harmlessly through the frame's grounding strap. One of those straps near station 2 has been loose for a while, so instead of discharging cleanly it sometimes arcs near the sensor wiring and causes a brief glitch. Check the grounding strap continuity with a multimeter, not just a visual check, because it can look connected but have a bad crimp. Re-terminate it and the dry-day glitches stop.
